title: karwinskia umbellata
intro: Karwinskia umbellata, also known as Copal, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Polygalaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Mexican folk medicine, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Karwinskia umbellata are attributed to its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Karwinskia umbellata is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by infusing the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
